message:
fix(fcp-mesh): wire gossip prune_stale_peers into MeshNode::prune_stale_state

MeshGossip::prune_stale_peers (gossip.rs:1588) existed but was only
called from tests. MeshNode::prune_stale_state — the central production
cleanup entry point that already prunes symbol_requests, sent_symbols,
and admission peers — was missing it. Each handle_summary() call
inserts or refreshes a PeerGossipState entry in the gossip peer_states
HashMap, so without periodic pruning the map grew with every peer the
node ever interacted with.

Patch: add self.gossip.prune_stale_peers(now_ms / 1000) alongside the
existing prune calls. The /1000 conversion matches the convention
elsewhere in node.rs — gossip timestamps are in seconds, node state
is in milliseconds.

Verification pending upstream: rch + local both blocked by pre-existing
asupersync compile errors (unchanged blocker). The patch is a trivial
single-line addition that only funnels an already-public pruning
routine through the already-public prune surface.

Found during SunnyMoose fresh-eyes audit (bead 2ot4f, 2026-04-17).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
